response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/JavaScript and DLang expertise, but fundamentally lacks the core qualifications for this JVM-focused role. His Java experience is minimal and incidental — brief usage at Altom for robotic arms and a short C IDE stint at National Instruments — with no Kotlin, no Spring ecosystem work, and no JVM internals knowledge evident across 15+ years of professional experience. His developer tooling background is tangentially relevant but does not compensate for the absence of the role's primary technical requirements.
strengths: Strong developer tooling experience including test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts) and MCP code generators
Proven track record with high-performance, large-scale systems handling millions of requests per second
Consistent software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active knowledge-sharing
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only incidental Java usage across career — primary stack is TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang
Zero Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience (no Quarkus, Hibernate, or equivalent) mentioned anywhere
No JVM internals or IDE tooling depth; NI LabWindows work was C-based, not JVM-related
